candidates.title: The Absorption Tax: How Feature Hierarchy Structure Governs SAE Failure Modes Across Semantic Domains
candidates.summary: Evidence-grounded revision enriched by 6 fresh perspectives. PRIMARY: First systematic cross-domain absorption characterization with the striking pilot finding that first-letter spelling shows the LOWEST absorption (3.9%), while semantic hierarchies show substantially higher rates (city-language 10.4%, city-continent up to 53.4%). SECONDARY: Causal absorption confirmation via activation patching (14.3% recovery vs 0.5% control) + NEW benign-vs-pathological diagnostic from Contrarian perspective. TERTIARY: Rate-distortion three-factor predictor framework (from Theoretical + Interdisciplinary). NEGATIVE RESULTS: GAS unsupervised detector fails (rho=0.12), CMI at L0=22 not supported (rho=0.044), absorption tax quantitative predictions weak.
hypotheses: H1: Cross-domain absorption rates differ significantly from first-letter (PARTIALLY SUPPORTED, p=0.005 for city-language)
H2': Semantic hierarchies show HIGHER absorption than syntactic first-letter (SUPPORTED by pilot, inverting original H2)
H3: Absorption-hedging ratio varies by hierarchy type (SUPPORTED: 69/31 for city-continent vs 45/55 for first-letter)
H5: Absorption tax quantitative predictions (NOT SUPPORTED: rho=0.08 for absorption-MSE)
H6: Architecture ranking consistent across hierarchies (INCONCLUSIVE: JumpReLU consistently best, Matryoshka mixed)
H7: Causal absorption via activation patching (SUPPORTED: 14.3% recovery vs 0.5% control, needs larger n)
H8: Benign vs. pathological absorption (NEW, UNTESTED, from Contrarian: >= 30% benign expected)
H9: Rate-distortion three-factor predictor (NEW, UNTESTED, from Theoretical + Interdisciplinary: rho > 0.5 target)
candidates.pilot_focus: COMPLETED. 11 of 13 tasks done. Key findings: H2 falsified (first-letter NOT worst case), H4 refuted (GAS fails), H5 not supported. Three blocking experiments completed. New hypotheses H8, H9 untested.
candidates.key_novelty: Cross-domain characterization (9/10). Causal absorption via activation patching (8/10). Benign-vs-pathological diagnostic (8/10, NEW). Tightened hedging classification (8/10). Rate-distortion predictors (7/10, NEW). Absorption Tax qualitative (7/10).
candidates.crowded_risks: No competing cross-domain work found as of April 2026 (verified via arXiv, Google Scholar, web search). GAS failure means no unsupervised detection claim. Field velocity high for architectural mitigations but not for characterization.
candidates.compute_estimate: ~10.5 GPU-hours total. Phase 1 (cross-domain + probes): 6.5 hr. Phase 2 (activation patching + benign/pathological): 2 hr. Phase 3 (rate-distortion predictors): 1 hr. Phase 4 (negative results): 1 hr CPU.
candidates.falsification_conditions: Probe quality improvement reverses H2' finding AND activation patching effect disappears at larger n AND benign/pathological ratio is uninformative. All three would need to occur simultaneously to invalidate core claims.
candidates.blocking_dependency: Probe quality improvement at layers 18/24 for RAVEL hierarchies. If all probes remain below F1=0.85, fall back to GPT-2 Small.
candidates.venue_target: Primary: NeurIPS 2026 / ICLR 2027 main. Fallback: EMNLP 2026 / NeurIPS MI Workshop.
key_changes_from_prior_round: H8 (benign-vs-pathological) added from Contrarian perspective -- converts philosophical debate into empirical measurement
H9 (rate-distortion three-factor predictor) added from Theoretical + Interdisciplinary -- tests cos_sim x co-occur / R
Ecological niche competition framework integrated as intuitive narrative (Interdisciplinary)
MIG/completeness framework from VAEs retained as theoretical motivation for future unsupervised work (Innovator)
Statistical rigor requirements strengthened: Bonferroni correction, random-baseline controls (Empiricist)
Paper structure remains two-contribution based on evidence, with H8 and H9 as enrichments not new primary claims
All 6 fresh perspectives independently validated cross-domain characterization as highest priority

candidates.title: Controlled Dictionary Experiment: Separating Encoder from Dictionary in Absorption Causation
candidates.summary: Hold decoder dictionary constant, vary encoder (feedforward vs. OMP vs. 2-pass). Tests whether absorption is encoder or dictionary failure. Strengthened by pilot activation patching result confirming encoder-level competitive exclusion.
hypotheses: If amortization gap dominates: OMP absorption < 50% of feedforward at matched L0
If sparsity landscape dominates: absorption similar across encoding methods
candidates.pilot_focus: Feedforward vs. OMP comparison on 1000 tokens, Gemma Scope L12-16k. 30 min.
candidates.key_novelty: 9/10. First controlled dictionary experiment. MP-SAE does not hold dictionary constant.
candidates.pivot_trigger: Activate if cross-domain probes fail AND activation patching effect disappears. Always valuable as supplementary.

candidates.title: Feature Absorption as Competitive Exclusion: Phase Transitions and Limiting Similarity
candidates.summary: Formalize absorption through ecological Lotka-Volterra competition and rate-distortion phase transition theory. STRENGTHENED by: (1) pilot evidence showing hierarchy-dependent absorption rates (predicted by the model), (2) Interdisciplinary perspective's phase-transition framework, (3) Theoretical perspective's rate-distortion bound. Predict sharp phase transition at critical cosine similarity theta*.
hypotheses: Phase transition: absorption probability sigmoid with transition width < 0.1
Hierarchy-dependent theta*: first-letter < city-language < city-continent (matching pilot ordering)
Universal gamma = 1/2 (mean-field, from Interdisciplinary Ambrogioni et al.)
candidates.pilot_focus: Plot absorption probability vs. decoder cosine similarity using Phase 1 data. 15 min CPU.
candidates.key_novelty: 9/10. Zero prior work on competitive exclusion or phase transitions in SAE absorption.
candidates.pivot_trigger: Activate as supplementary analysis when Phase 1 data is available. Provides theoretical explanation for WHY cross-domain rates differ. Particularly strong if H9 (three-factor predictor) succeeds.

candidates.title: Absorption-Aware Post-Hoc Feature Correction
candidates.summary: Given known absorption patterns, construct corrected feature activations by re-activating absorbed parent features when child fires. Tests whether absorption can be handled at inference time without architectural changes.
hypotheses: Post-hoc correction recovers >= 50% of SAE-vs-probe gap on downstream tasks
Or: correction has negligible effect because absorption permanently rewires decoder direction
candidates.pilot_focus: Apply correction to first-letter absorption using known absorption patterns. 30 min.
candidates.key_novelty: 8/10. First evaluation of post-hoc absorption correction.
candidates.pivot_trigger: Activate if reviewers ask for prescriptive guidance or if H8 shows most absorption is pathological.

dropped_candidates.title: GAS as Primary Unsupervised Absorption Detector
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: REFUTED by pilot: Spearman rho=0.12 (target 0.6, failure threshold 0.3). Decoder-activation co-occurrence mismatch does not capture absorption.

dropped_candidates.title: Information-Theoretic Absorption Taxonomy via CMI
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: NOT SUPPORTED by pilot: rho=0.044 (p=0.83) at L0=22. CMI does not predict absorption.

dropped_candidates.title: Absorption Tax with Quantitative Predictions (T(G), R_pc)
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Quantitative predictions not supported: absorption-MSE rho=0.08 (p=0.87), R_pc rho=0.16 (p=0.46). Retained as qualitative framework only.

dropped_candidates.title: EDA as Universal Weight-Only Absorption Detector
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Works in specific regimes but fails for 75% early absorption. Replaced by GAS, which also failed.

dropped_candidates.title: ITAC as Primary Absorption Detection Method (Innovator Perspective)
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Core unsupervised hypothesis refuted by GAS failure (rho=0.12). Conditional independence component (ITAC Component 2) untested but deprioritized given geometric component failure. Retained as Backup 4 for potential future exploration.

dropped_candidates.title: Hierarchy-Coherent SAE Loss
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Crowded by Muchane et al., Luo et al. HSAE, KronSAE. Requires SAE retraining.

dropped_candidates.title: Absorption Scaling Laws: Parametric Predictions from SAEBench Grid
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Novelty 5/10. Incorporated into Phase 3 rate-distortion predictor analysis rather than standalone paper.

dropped_candidates.title: Absorption as Implicit Hierarchical Regularization (PAC-Bayes)
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: From Theoretical perspective. Bound improvement negligibly small for practical n. Tangential to community interests. Dropped after self-critique.
